insights:
The single best deal is DeepSeek V4 Flash, which delivers an unbeatable combination of capability and cost. With an intelligence score of 40 out of 60, a blended price of just $0.06 per million tokens, and a value score of 666.7 — far higher than any other model — it offers solid reasoning performance for production workloads. Its output speed of 106 tokens per second and massive 1,000K context window support long, complex interactions without slow down. The Arena ELO of 1,435 confirms competent real-world performance, making DeepSeek V4 Flash the clear choice for developers seeking maximum utility per dollar.

Notable runners-up present specific trade-offs. MiMo-V2-Flash shares the same $0.06 price but scores only 25 on intelligence and offers a smaller 256K context, limiting its usefulness for advanced reasoning or long documents. DeepSeek V4 Pro raises intelligence to 44 and Arena ELO to 1,457, but triples the price to $0.18 and reduces speed to 90 t/s; its value score falls to 244.4. Xiaomi MiMo-V2.5-Pro offers comparable intelligence at the same $0.18 price but is slower at 53 t/s and has a narrower reach in benchmarks. MiniMax M3 matches DeepSeek V4 Pro’s intelligence of 44 at $0.22, with excellent code ability (Arena Code ELO 1,505), but costs 3.7× more than the flash model. StepFun Step 3.7 Flash offers blistering speed at 393 t/s and moderate intelligence of 30, making it viable for high-throughput but less demanding tasks.

For the most common use case — cost-sensitive API calls in production — the actionable recommendation is to default to DeepSeek V4 Flash. It dominates the value landscape while still providing sufficient intelligence for a wide range of applications, from chatbots to retrieval-augmented generation. If occasional higher reasoning quality is essential for complex edge cases, fall back to DeepSeek V4 Pro or MiniMax M3, but reserve them for only those queries to keep overall spending low.
